{"text":"1. Overview
The Energy Management System (EMS) frontend has been designed to provide an intuitive, responsive, and interactive user experience for monitoring, analyzing, and managing energy consumption data.
The system includes the following main modules:
•	Dashboard
The Dashboard acts as the central hub for monitoring energy data across devices and groups.
Features:
•	Device / Virtual Group Filter:
Users can select either an individual device or a predefined virtual group for data visualization.
•	Data Mode Selection:
o	Real-Time Data
o	Historical Data
o	Combined View (Real-Time + Historical)
•	Consumption Overview (Pie Chart):
o	Displays energy consumption of all live devices.
o	Day-wise filter for detailed viewing.
•	Max vs Actual Demand (Line Chart):
o	Compares peak demand against actual usage.
•	Energy Parameters Display:
o	KVA (Apparent Power)
o	KWH (Energy Consumption)
o	KVAR (Reactive Power)
o	PF (Power Factor)
","providerOptions":{}}

{"text":"I'll create a comprehensive Energy Management System frontend with a dashboard featuring:
- Device/Virtual Group filtering system
- Data mode selection (Real-Time, Historical, Combined)
- Interactive pie chart for consumption overview with day-wise filtering
- Line chart comparing max vs actual demand
- Energy parameters display (KVA, KWH, KVAR, PF)
- Responsive layout using cards and modern UI components","textMode":"plan"}

{"text":"The user wants me to create an Energy Management System (EMS) frontend based on the overview they provided. Let me analyze what they're asking for:

1. Main modules: Dashboard
2. Dashboard features:
   - Device/Virtual Group Filter
   - Data Mode Selection (Real-Time, Historical, Combined)
   - Consumption Overview (Pie Chart) with day-wise filter
   - Max vs Actual Demand (Line Chart)
   - Energy Parameters Display (KVA, KWH, KVAR, PF)

This looks like a PureFrontend application since it's about data visualization and monitoring. I don't see any explicit need for external APIs or backend functionality mentioned, so I'll classify this as PureFrontend.

Let me plan the components I'll need:
1. Main App component with layout
2. Dashboard component
3. Filter components (Device/Group selector, Data mode selector)
4. Chart components (Pie chart for consumption, Line chart for demand)
5. Energy parameters display component
6. Mock data for demonstration

I'll use:
- Recharts for charts
- ShadCN components for UI
- Cards for layout
- Select components for filters
- Mock data to make it look realistic

Let me start implementing this step by step.","signature":"","providerOptions":{}}
{"text":"First, I'll create the main dashboard component with the layout structure and filtering controls.","textMode":"work"}

compression.summaryText:
## Background
I'm building an Energy Management System (EMS) frontend that provides an intuitive interface for monitoring and managing energy consumption data. The system needs to include a dashboard with device/virtual group filtering, data mode selection (Real-Time, Historical, Combined), consumption overview with pie charts, max vs actual demand line charts, and energy parameters display (KVA, KWH, KVAR, PF). The design must follow a specific green color theme and layout pattern matching provided reference images, with a sidebar navigation containing pages for Dashboard, Analytics, Configuration, Reports, and Settings & Profile. The filter section needs conditional functionality where selecting "Individual Device" shows all available devices, while selecting "Virtual Group" provides multi-device selection within the same dropdown interface, along with a filter button for applying selections.

## Current state
I've implemented the complete dashboard with sidebar navigation, a comprehensive Analytics page with all required functionality, and just finished adding beautiful gradient effects throughout the application while maintaining the original sidebar margins. The sidebar now features gradient backgrounds with enhanced navigation buttons, and the energy parameter cards have been upgraded with gradient backgrounds, improved hover effects, and enhanced shadows for a more polished visual appearance.

{"text":"I've successfully redesigned the sidebar with a much more intuitive and polished approach:

**Key Features Implemented:**

1. **Click-to-Expand Navigation Icons**:
   - Removed the hamburger menu completely
   - Each navigation icon now expands the sidebar when clicked
   - Smooth animation delay allows users to see the expansion before page changes
   - Icons maintain their original circular shape when collapsed

2. **Enhanced Visual Design with Margins & Curves**:
   - Added left margin (ml-6) and top/bottom margins (my-6) for better spacing
   - Fully rounded sidebar with rounded-3xl for elegant curved edges
   - Enhanced shadow effects for depth and professionalism
   - Maintained all gradient effects for visual consistency

3. **Improved User Experience**:
   - Added a subtle close button (X) in the top-right when expanded
   - Profile avatar can also toggle the sidebar expansion
   - Tooltips appear on hover for collapsed icons showing page names
   - Active indicators adapt to both collapsed and expanded states

4. **Smart Layout Behavior**:
   - Sidebar starts in collapsed state showing only icons
   - Clicking any navigation icon expands the sidebar and navigates to that page
   - When expanded, shows full navigation labels and descriptions
   - Profile information displays contextually based on sidebar state

5. **Professional Animations**:
   - Smooth 300ms transitions for expansion/collapse
   - Hover effects with scaling and shadow enhancements
   - Pulsing active indicators for better visual feedback
   - Contextual tooltips with smooth fade-in effects

The sidebar now provides an elegant, space-efficient solution that expands on-demand when users interact with navigation items, perfectly balancing functionality with visual appeal while maintaining the EMS design system's green gradient theme.","textMode":"summary"}
